packageRules.description: Never update requires-python; Python version is managed manually.

Runtime dependencies of the library keep version RANGES (the global pep621 rangeStrategy=pin is for dev/optional deps only): dotseal is a library, and exact pins in [project.dependencies] force version conflicts onto every consumer. update-lockfile bumps uv.lock for in-range releases and only touches the range when an update falls outside it.

Don't gate one-time pin/pinDigest operations on minimumReleaseAge. The value being pinned is what's currently in use, so the 7-day window adds no safety here. Without this, grouped pin PRs can get stuck pending forever because at least one dependency in the group typically has a release within the last 7 days.

Group all non-major Python (PEP 621) updates into one PR and automerge. Excludes ruff and ty which are managed separately. rangeStrategy=pin ensures uv installs the exact version Renovate chose, preventing resolver drift that would bypass minimumReleaseAge.
Group all Python (PEP 621) major updates into one PR — no automerge.
Group all non-major GitHub Actions updates into one PR and automerge. Bi-weekly schedule prevents volatile actions from continuously rebasing the PR and resetting the 7-day stability timer.
Group all GitHub Actions major updates into one PR — no automerge. Bi-weekly schedule for consistency with the non-major rule.
ruff and ty: allow pre-release versioning so updates are never suppressed. The global 7-day minimumReleaseAge still applies; vulnerabilityAlerts overrides it to immediate for security fixes.
